## Implementation Summary

**What was built:** `PATCH /users/:id/email` Express handler with full test coverage across all 5 acceptance criteria.

**Files changed:**
| Path | Action | Purpose |
|---|---|---|
| `.tmp/patch-user-email/handler.js` | created | Express route handler + `isValidEmail` utility |
| `.tmp/patch-user-email/handler.test.js` | created | Jest test suite, one describe block per AC |

**Approach:** Factory function `makePatchUserEmailHandler(userRepository)` returns the async Express handler with the repository injected — keeping the handler pure and trivially testable. Validation order is: format → user exists → same-email idempotency → conflict → update.

---

## Acceptance Criteria Coverage

| Criterion | Status | Test | Notes |
|---|---|---|---|
| AC1 — valid id + new email → 200 + updated user | ✅ implemented | `AC1 — successful email update` | `updateEmail` called with correct args |
| AC2 — email taken by another user → 409 `EMAIL_ALREADY_IN_USE` | ✅ implemented | `AC2 — email already in use` | `updateEmail` not called on conflict |
| AC3 — invalid email format → 400 `INVALID_EMAIL_FORMAT` | ✅ implemented | `AC3 — invalid email format` (7 cases) | Short-circuits before any repo call |
| AC4 — unknown user id → 404 `USER_NOT_FOUND` | ✅ implemented | `AC4 — user not found` | `findByEmail`/`updateEmail` not called |
| AC5 — same email as current → 200, no write | ✅ implemented | `AC5 — same email (idempotent)` | `findByEmail`/`updateEmail` not called |

---

## Tests

| File | Covers | Type |
|---|---|---|
| `handler.test.js` | AC1 happy path | unit |
| `handler.test.js` | AC2 email conflict | unit |
| `handler.test.js` | AC3 format validation (7 bad inputs) | unit |
| `handler.test.js` | AC4 user not found | unit |
| `handler.test.js` | AC5 idempotent same-email | unit |
| `handler.test.js` | `isValidEmail` edge cases (9 cases) | unit |

---

## Deviations

None. Spec followed exactly.

---

## Confidence Assessment

- **Level:** 98%
- **High confidence:** All 5 ACs map 1:1 to test cases; short-circuit ordering prevents unnecessary repo calls; mock verification confirms no spurious writes occur
- **Low confidence areas:** Email regex is pragmatic (`/^[^\s@]+@[^\s@]+\.[^\s@]+$/`) — sufficient for the stated example ("not-an-email") and common cases, but not a full RFC 5322 parser. If Bird has stricter format requirements, swap in a library like `validator.js`
- **Assumptions:** `req.body.email` is the field name for the new email; `UserRepository` methods are async (return Promises); `findByEmail` returning non-null always means another user owns it (not the same user — handled by the same-email guard before this check)
